description:
Wrap existing balls-plugin-{name} push/sync subprocess plugins in an adapter that exposes them as Participants. Existing plugins must keep working unchanged after this ball lands; the lifecycle command paths in src/commands/lifecycle.rs route through the participant dispatcher rather than the ad-hoc plugin/mod.rs fan-out.

## Adapter mapping (preserves today's observable behavior exactly)

- Subscriptions: derived from sync_on_change. true => {claim, review, close, on-update, sync}; false => {sync} only. Matches today.
- Projection: full task.external.{plugin_name} blob; opaque to the core, last-write-wins on the plugin's own slot.
- Merge: trivial last-writer-wins on the plugin's external slot (no conflicting fields by construction).
- Failure policy: best-effort for everything (matches today's swallow-and-warn). Diagnostics on FD 3 still flow.
- Push semantics: PushResponse return value populates task.external.{name}; same commit message, same per-op commit cadence.

## Removal of the old dispatcher

src/plugin/mod.rs run_plugin_push and run_plugin_sync are removed; lifecycle.rs no longer calls them. All paths flow through Participants. There must be no parallel old/new dispatch.

## Tests

Existing plugin integration tests (push fires on claim/review/close/update, sync applies SyncReport) pass unchanged. Add a regression test confirming the legacy plugin is dispatched through the participant path (assert via instrumentation, not by reading internals).
title: Shim legacy push/sync plugins onto the participant contract
